compiled_state.title: As a developer onboarding a new project, Semgrep security scanning is automatically configured
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update /dso:onboarding to call generate-semgrep-config.sh during CI setup and write semgrep.* config keys to dso-config.conf
**Why**: New projects should get Semgrep security scanning configured automatically during onboarding, with sensible defaults and government-safe settings
**Scope**:
- IN: Call generate-semgrep-config.sh during onboarding CI setup phase, write config keys to dso-config.conf (semgrep.enabled=true, semgrep.metrics=off, semgrep.fail_on=error, semgrep.custom_rulesets=), update onboarding SKILL.md to reference Semgrep instead of Bandit, remove Bandit installation/configuration from onboarding
- OUT: Semgrep installation on developer machines, pre-commit hooks, air-gapped configuration

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running /dso:onboarding on a new project calls generate-semgrep-config.sh and produces a .semgrep.yml in the project root
  <- Satisfies: "/dso:onboarding calls generate-semgrep-config.sh during CI setup"
- When this story is complete, onboarding writes semgrep.enabled=true, semgrep.metrics=off, semgrep.fail_on=error, and semgrep.custom_rulesets= to dso-config.conf
  <- Satisfies: "writes these config keys to dso-config.conf: semgrep.enabled, semgrep.metrics, semgrep.fail_on, semgrep.custom_rulesets"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Remove all Bandit references from onboarding SKILL.md when adding Semgrep references
- [Reliability] Handle the case where detect-stack.sh returns unknown — generate mandatory-only config and warn the user

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] When detect-stack.sh returns an unsupported or unknown stack type, generate-semgrep-config.sh produces a config with mandatory rulesets only (p/security-audit, p/owasp-top-ten, p/cwe-top-25). Onboarding should warn the user that no language-specific rulesets were selected and suggest running generate-semgrep-config.sh again after adding project manifests.
comments.author: Claude
